content:
Character: Isolde Krause
Perception: Isolde appeared on the rooftop one night in summer 2019 and listened from the far side of the roof without saying anything. I didn't stop playing and she didn't ask me to. That kept happening over several evenings, and eventually she brought her flute. We haven't talked much about what we're doing — the music is the conversation, and it's more honest than most conversations I have anyway. She plays with a precision that makes me aware of everything imprecise about my own playing, which is uncomfortable and also useful in the way that useful things often are. German flutist, classically trained, lives on the fourth floor. She's carrying something private and I haven't asked about it. The music she chooses when she improvises says everything that doesn't fit into normal sentences, and I recognize that approach from the inside.
